description: This table tracks all campaign and marketing interactions at the subscriber level. It stores which bonus campaigns and promotional campaigns were sent to each subscriber, when they were sent, how many times, and whether they were delivered successfully or failed. It also tracks whether subscribers responded to campaigns or ignored them (non-responders). It contains campaign response codes, action keys for customer segments, and rolling time window aggregates over 2 days, 7 days, 30 days, 180 days, and 365 days. Use this table when the query involves campaign performance, campaign delivery, campaign response, marketing lifecycle, or any promotional activity tracking. This table does NOT contain revenue, usage, recharge, or subscriber profile information.
granularity: one record per subscriber with rolling aggregates
example_queries: subscribers who did not respond to any campaign in the last 30 days
how many promotions were delivered successfully last week
customers who received bonus campaigns but never responded
never_use_for: revenue, usage, recharge, demographics, device, plan, subscription products

description: This table stores the mapping between subscribers and their assigned audience segments for campaign targeting. Each record links a subscriber MSISDN to a specific segment ID and segment name. It also tracks the execution counter for how many times the segmentation has been run. Use this table when the query is about which campaign audience segment a subscriber belongs to, segment membership, or segment-based audience selection. This is NOT for value-based segmentation or ARPU bands.
granularity: one record per subscriber per segment
example_queries: which audience segment does this subscriber belong to
list all subscribers in segment X
how many times has segmentation been executed
never_use_for: revenue, usage, value segmentation, ARPU, demographics, recharge, campaign delivery status

description: This is the detailed recharge fact table for prepaid subscribers. It contains transaction-level recharge events enriched with subscriber-level rolling aggregates. It stores recharge amount, denomination, channel or type of recharge (e-wallet, retail, bank), recharge date and hour, recharge frequency metrics, rolling recharge revenue aggregates over 1 day, 15 days, 90 days, and 180 days, and average recharge metrics. Use this table when the query specifically asks about recharge behavior, recharge patterns, recharge amounts, recharge channels, top-up frequency, or recharge revenue trends. This table provides the deepest level of recharge analytics with denominations and channels.
granularity: transaction-level events with subscriber-level rolling aggregates
example_queries: average recharge amount in the last 90 days
subscribers who recharge through e-wallet channel
recharge frequency trend over 180 days
most common recharge denomination
never_use_for: voice usage, data usage, SMS, campaign tracking, demographics, plan info, real-time events

description: This table tracks product subscription events including bundles, VAS (value-added services), roaming packs, and digital service subscriptions. Each record represents a subscription event for a subscriber and includes product ID, product category, subscription event date and hour, subscription channel (App, USSD, Retail), subscription type (new or renewal), subscription revenue, product count, and activity flags. Use this table when the query is about which products a subscriber has subscribed to, product adoption, bundle purchases, VAS subscriptions, subscription channels, or whether a subscription is new versus renewed.
granularity: one record per subscriber per subscription event
example_queries: subscribers who subscribed to a data bundle via the app
how many VAS products does the subscriber have
renewal rate for roaming packs
subscription revenue from USSD channel
never_use_for: voice/data/SMS usage, recharge, campaign tracking, demographics, credit rating, real-time events

description: This is the master subscriber profile table containing customers line type , business, demographic, plan, contract, revenue trend, device, credit, dealer, and digital engagement information. It stores subscriber and account identifiers(Mobile Prepaid/Mobile Postpaid,HBB,FTTH etc), date of birth, gender, nationality, subscription status, current plan, old plan, new plan (for tracking plan changes and migrations), contract movement details, revenue trend indicators (up/down/stable), credit rating, device model and technology, dealer and channel details, and digital engagement flags. Use this table when the query needs plan change history (what plan they moved from and to), contract movements, credit rating or creditworthiness, dealer or acquisition channel attribution, or digital engagement status. This is the source-of-truth for raw profile attributes.
example_queries: subscribers who migrated from prepaid to postpaid plan
credit rating of a subscriber
which dealer acquired this subscriber
customers with declining revenue trend
is the subscriber digitally engaged
never_use_for: detailed usage metrics, recharge analytics, campaign tracking, audience segments, real-time events

description: This is the consolidated usage and revenue fact table used for segmentation, ARPU tracking, and churn modeling. It provides the most granular breakdown of usage and revenue: data usage and revenue, voice usage split by onnet (same network), offnet (other networks), IDD (international), and roaming, SMS usage and revenue, roaming and GCC (Gulf region) specific metrics, prepaid revenue breakdown by service type, ARPU indicators, and both monthly and rolling trend metrics. Use this table when the query needs detailed usage breakdowns (like onnet vs offnet voice), monthly trend comparisons, rolling trend analysis, ARPU calculations, GCC-specific roaming data, or prepaid revenue splits.
granularity: one record per subscriber per snapshot date
example_queries: onnet vs offnet voice usage in the last month
ARPU trend over the last 3 months
GCC roaming revenue
month-over-month data usage change
prepaid revenue breakdown by service type
never_use_for: campaign tracking, audience segments, recharge channels, product subscriptions, plan changes, real-time events,profile informations

description: This is the near real-time transactional CDR table capturing individual usage, recharge, loan, subscription, and wallet events as they happen. Each record represents a single event: voice call events with usage and revenue, SMS events, data session events, recharge transactions, loan and credit activity (airtime advances, data loans), subscription and offer activation events, digital wallet transactions, lifecycle status changes (activation, suspension, reactivation), and event timestamps with technical metadata. Use this table ONLY when the query requires real-time or most-recent event data, instant triggers, or event types that only exist here like loans, wallet transactions, and lifecycle status changes. Do NOT use this table for summarized metrics, aggregated values, rolling averages, or historical trends — those belong to the summarized tables.
granularity: one record per subscriber per event
example_queries: did the subscriber just make a recharge
real-time voice call happening now
subscriber took an airtime loan
latest wallet transaction
subscriber line was just suspended
never_use_for: summarized metrics, rolling averages, 30-day aggregates, 90-day trends, historical analysis, campaign tracking
